related_clusters.name: Boolean State Cluster
related_clusters.relationship: Configuration cluster for Boolean State sensor

cluster_properties.safety_classification: MEDIUM - Alert/Notification Device (Contact Sensors, Water Leak, Freeze Detect)

SPRS.description: Supports ability to suppress or acknowledge alarms

SENSLVL.description: Supports ability to set sensor sensitivity
SENSLVL.security_note: Can affect detection threshold - potential for false negatives

properties.name: SuppressAlarm_Requires_Operate_Privilege
properties.claim: SuppressAlarm command SHALL require Operate (O) privilege to execute.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7, Page 94: Command table shows Access: O for SuppressAlarm

violation.description: If SuppressAlarm could be invoked without Operate privilege, a View-only user could suppress active alarms. This would allow unauthorized silencing of safety alerts.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker with View privilege attempts to invoke SuppressAlarm command
violation.physical_impact: Users may miss critical alerts (water leak, contact sensor breach)

properties.proverifQuery: query invoker:principal, alarm:alarmMode ⊢ event suppress_alarm(invoker, alarm) ==> has_privilege(invoker, Operate)

properties.name: EnableDisableAlarm_Requires_Operate_Privilege
properties.claim: EnableDisableAlarm command SHALL require Operate (O) privilege to execute.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7, Page 94: Command table shows Access: O for EnableDisableAlarm

violation.description: If EnableDisableAlarm could be invoked without Operate privilege, a View-only user could disable all alarm functionality. This is more severe than suppression as it completely disables alarms.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker with View privilege attempts to disable all alarm modes
violation.physical_impact: Complete loss of alarm notification capability for water leaks, contact sensor breaches

properties.proverifQuery: query invoker:principal, alarms:alarmBitmap ⊢ event enable_disable_alarm(invoker, alarms) ==> has_privilege(invoker, Operate)

properties.name: CurrentSensitivityLevel_Write_Requires_Operate
properties.claim: Writing to CurrentSensitivityLevel attribute SHALL require View and Operate (VO) privilege.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6, Page 91: Attribute table shows Access: RW VO for CurrentSensitivityLevel

violation.description: If sensitivity could be changed without Operate privilege, an attacker could reduce sensor sensitivity causing missed detections (false negatives).
violation.attack_vector: Attacker with View privilege attempts to write low sensitivity value
violation.physical_impact: Sensor may fail to detect triggering conditions (water, movement, contact)

properties.proverifQuery: query writer:principal, level:uint8 ⊢ event write_sensitivity(writer, level) ==> has_privilege(writer, Operate)

properties.name: AlarmsActive_Read_Only
properties.claim: AlarmsActive attribute SHALL be read-only (View access only, no write capability).
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6, Page 92: Access: RV (Read View only)

violation.description: If AlarmsActive could be written remotely, an attacker could clear active alarms without addressing the underlying trigger, or set false alarms.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker attempts to write 0x00 to AlarmsActive to clear active alarms
violation.physical_impact: Active alarms silently cleared without user awareness

properties.proverifQuery: query writer:principal, value:bitmap ⊢ ¬event write_alarms_active(writer, value)

properties.name: AlarmsSuppressed_Read_Only
properties.claim: AlarmsSuppressed attribute SHALL be read-only (View access only, modified only via SuppressAlarm command).
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6, Page 92: Access: RV (Read View only)

violation.description: Direct write to AlarmsSuppressed would bypass command validation logic (active alarm check).
violation.attack_vector: Attacker attempts to directly write suppression state without command invocation
violation.physical_impact: Suppression state manipulation without proper state validation

properties.proverifQuery: query writer:principal, value:bitmap ⊢ ¬event direct_write_suppressed(writer, value)

properties.name: AlarmsEnabled_Read_Only
properties.claim: AlarmsEnabled attribute SHALL be read-only (View access only, modified only via EnableDisableAlarm command).
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6, Page 92: Access: RV (Read View only)

violation.description: Direct write to AlarmsEnabled would bypass command validation logic.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker attempts to directly disable alarms without going through EnableDisableAlarm command
violation.physical_impact: Alarms disabled without proper state transition handling

properties.proverifQuery: query writer:principal, value:bitmap ⊢ ¬event direct_write_enabled(writer, value)

properties.name: AlarmsActive_Unsupported_Bits_Zero
properties.claim: Bits in AlarmsActive for unsupported alarm modes SHALL always be 0.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6.4, Page 93: 'If an alarm mode is not supported, the bit indicating this alarm mode SHALL always be 0.'

violation.description: If unsupported alarm bits could be set in AlarmsActive, it would create inconsistent state that clients cannot interpret correctly.
violation.attack_vector: Implementation bug or fault condition allowing unsupported bits to be set
violation.physical_impact: None directly - state consistency issue

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, bit:alarmBit ⊢ ¬supported(device, bit) ==> active_bit(device, bit) == false

properties.name: AlarmsSuppressed_Unsupported_Bits_Zero
properties.claim: Bits in AlarmsSuppressed for unsupported alarm modes SHALL always be 0.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6.5, Page 93: 'If an alarm mode is not supported, the bit indicating this alarm mode SHALL always be 0.'

violation.description: If unsupported alarm bits could be set in AlarmsSuppressed, it would indicate suppression of non-existent alarm mode.
violation.attack_vector: Implementation bug allowing unsupported bits in suppressed state
violation.physical_impact: None directly - state consistency issue

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, bit:alarmBit ⊢ ¬supported(device, bit) ==> suppressed_bit(device, bit) == false

properties.name: Suppression_Requires_Active_Alarm
properties.claim: SuppressAlarm command SHALL only succeed if the specified alarm modes are currently active.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.1.2, Page 94: 'If any of the requested alarm modes are not active, in case the sensor is not triggered or the alarm mode is disabled/not supported, this command SHALL be ignored and the server SHALL return an INVALID_IN_STATE status code.'

violation.description: Allowing suppression of inactive alarms would create inconsistent state where suppressed bit is set but active bit is not.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker sends SuppressAlarm for inactive alarm mode attempting to corrupt state
violation.physical_impact: None - server should return INVALID_IN_STATE

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, bit:alarmBit ⊢ event suppress_success(device, bit) ==> active_bit(device, bit) == true

properties.name: Suppress_Unsupported_Returns_Error
properties.claim: SuppressAlarm command with unsupported alarm modes SHALL be ignored and return CONSTRAINT_ERROR.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.1.2, Page 94: 'If any of the requested alarm modes are not supported this command SHALL be ignored and the server SHALL return an CONSTRAINT_ERROR status code.'

violation.description: Accepting suppression of unsupported alarms would indicate implementation incorrectly processes invalid requests.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker sends SuppressAlarm with invalid/unsupported alarm bits
violation.physical_impact: None - error handling issue

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, cmd:command ⊢ unsupported_bit_in(cmd) ==> event return_error(device, cmd, CONSTRAINT_ERROR)

properties.name: EnableDisable_Unsupported_Returns_Error
properties.claim: EnableDisableAlarm command with unsupported alarm modes SHALL be ignored and return CONSTRAINT_ERROR.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.2.2, Page 95: 'If any of the requested alarm modes are not supported this command SHALL be ignored and the server SHALL return an CONSTRAINT_ERROR status code.'

violation.description: Accepting enable/disable of unsupported alarms indicates implementation bug.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker sends EnableDisableAlarm with invalid alarm bits
violation.physical_impact: None - error handling issue

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, cmd:command ⊢ unsupported_bit_in(cmd) ==> event return_error(device, cmd, CONSTRAINT_ERROR)

properties.name: SensitivityLevel_Validated
properties.claim: CurrentSensitivityLevel write with invalid value SHALL return CONSTRAINT_ERROR.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6.1, Page 92: 'If a write interaction to this attribute contains an unsupported sensitivity value, a CONSTRAINT_ERROR status SHALL be returned.'

violation.description: Accepting out-of-range sensitivity values would corrupt device configuration.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker writes value >= SupportedSensitivityLevels
violation.physical_impact: Undefined sensor behavior

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, value:uint8 ⊢ value >= SupportedSensitivityLevels(device) ==> event write_rejected(device, CurrentSensitivityLevel, CONSTRAINT_ERROR)

properties.name: Suppressed_Alarm_Still_Active
properties.claim: A suppressed alarm SHALL remain marked as active in AlarmsActive attribute; suppression only affects physical output.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.4.1, Page 91: 'A suppressed alarm is still considered active and will remain so unless it is actively disabled or the triggering condition is not longer present.'

violation.description: If suppression cleared the active bit, controllers would lose visibility of the alarm condition. The alarm is still logically active even when physically suppressed.
violation.attack_vector: Implementation bug that clears AlarmsActive when suppressing
violation.physical_impact: Controllers and automations lose awareness of active alarm conditions

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, bit:alarmBit ⊢ suppressed_bit(device, bit) == true ==> active_bit(device, bit) == true

properties.name: Disable_Clears_Active_And_Suppressed
properties.claim: Disabling an alarm mode SHALL clear both active and suppressed states for that mode.
properties.formal: ∀t, bit. disable(bit, t) ⟹ (AlarmsActive[bit] == 0 ∧ AlarmsSuppressed[bit] == 0) at t+1
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.2.2, Page 95: 'If an alarm mode is being disabled, any alarm mode which is either in the active or suppressed state SHALL be cleared and the alarm mode SHALL be considered not active.'

violation.description: If disabled alarms retained active/suppressed state, it would create inconsistent state where alarm is disabled but still marked active.
violation.attack_vector: N/A - this is expected behavior specification
violation.physical_impact: None - defines expected state clearing behavior

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, bit:alarmBit ⊢ event disable_alarm(device, bit) ==> event clear_active_suppressed(device, bit)

properties.name: Remote_Suppression_Possible
properties.claim: Alarms CAN be suppressed remotely via SuppressAlarm command if SPRS feature is supported.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.1, Page 94: SuppressAlarm command with Access: O

violation.description: This is not a violation but a DESIGN CONCERN. Remote suppression of safety alarms (water leak, contact sensors) without physical presence is inherently risky. A compromised node with Operate privilege can suppress all active alarms.
violation.attack_vector: Compromised controller or malicious app with Operate privilege sends SuppressAlarm
violation.physical_impact: Users may not be physically alerted to water leaks, intrusions, or other sensor triggers

properties.proverifQuery: query attacker:principal ⊢ has_privilege(attacker, Operate) ∧ SPRS_supported ==> can_suppress(attacker)
properties.design_note: SPECIFICATION DESIGN ISSUE: No physical confirmation or out-of-band verification for remote suppression

properties.name: Remote_Disable_Possible
properties.claim: Alarms CAN be completely disabled remotely via EnableDisableAlarm command.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.2, Page 94: EnableDisableAlarm command with Access: O

violation.description: This is a CRITICAL DESIGN CONCERN. Unlike suppression, disabling alarms completely removes the alarm capability. A compromised node can disable all alarms AND clear active/suppressed states silently.
violation.attack_vector: Compromised controller sends EnableDisableAlarm with all alarm bits cleared (0x00)
violation.physical_impact: Complete loss of alarm notification capability. Water leak sensor, contact sensor become silent even when triggered.

properties.proverifQuery: query attacker:principal ⊢ has_privilege(attacker, Operate) ==> can_disable_all_alarms(attacker)
properties.design_note: SPECIFICATION DESIGN ISSUE: Disabling alarms with Operate privilege (not Manage/Admin) is low barrier. EnableDisableAlarm silently clears active/suppressed states.

properties.name: Sensitivity_Manipulation_Detection_Impact
properties.claim: Changing CurrentSensitivityLevel to lowest value (0) reduces detection capability.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6.2, Page 92: 'value of 0 SHALL be considered the lowest sensitivity level (least sensitive)'

violation.description: An attacker with Operate privilege can reduce sensor sensitivity to minimum, causing false negatives (missed detections) while appearing to leave the device functional.
violation.attack_vector: Write CurrentSensitivityLevel = 0 to reduce detection threshold
violation.physical_impact: Water leaks, contact sensor breaches may not trigger alarms

properties.proverifQuery: query attacker:principal ⊢ has_privilege(attacker, Operate) ==> can_reduce_sensitivity(attacker)

properties.name: No_Rate_Limiting_On_SuppressAlarm
properties.claim: There is NO specification requirement for rate limiting on SuppressAlarm commands.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.1, Pages 94: No rate limiting requirement specified

violation.description: Absence of rate limiting allows repeated suppression attempts without restriction. While suppression requires active alarm state, there is no protection against repeated command flooding.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker floods device with SuppressAlarm commands
violation.physical_impact: Potential DoS; each command requires processing even if alarm state unchanged

properties.proverifQuery: N/A - Specification gap

properties.name: No_Rate_Limiting_On_EnableDisableAlarm
properties.claim: There is NO specification requirement for rate limiting on EnableDisableAlarm commands.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.2, Pages 94-95: No rate limiting requirement specified

violation.description: Absence of rate limiting allows rapid enable/disable cycling. This could cause alarm state thrashing.
violation.attack_vector: Attacker rapidly toggles alarm enable/disable states
violation.physical_impact: Alarm state instability; potential user confusion

properties.proverifQuery: N/A - Specification gap

properties.name: AlarmsStateChanged_Event_On_State_Change
properties.claim: AlarmsStateChanged event SHALL be generated after any bits in AlarmsActive and/or AlarmsSuppressed attributes change.
properties.formal: ∀t. (Δ(AlarmsActive) ∨ Δ(AlarmsSuppressed)) at t ⟹ gen_event(AlarmsStateChanged) at t
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.8.1, Page 95: 'This event SHALL be generated after any bits in the AlarmsActive and/or AlarmsSuppressed attributes change.'

violation.description: If state change events were not generated, controllers would have no notification of alarm state changes and would need to poll constantly.
violation.attack_vector: Implementation bug suppressing event generation
violation.physical_impact: Controllers miss alarm notifications; delayed response to safety conditions

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId ⊢ event state_change(device, AlarmsActive) ∨ event state_change(device, AlarmsSuppressed) ==> event alarm_state_changed(device)

properties.name: SensorFault_Event_On_Fault
properties.claim: SensorFault event SHALL be generated when the device registers or clears a fault.
properties.formal: ∀t. Δ(SensorFault) at t ⟹ gen_event(SensorFault) at t
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.8.2, Page 96: 'This event SHALL be generated when the device registers or clears a fault.'

violation.description: If fault events were not generated, controllers would not be aware of sensor hardware failures.
violation.attack_vector: Implementation bug suppressing fault event generation
violation.physical_impact: Users unaware of sensor malfunction; false sense of security

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId ⊢ event fault_change(device) ==> event sensor_fault_event(device)

properties.name: Event_Contains_Current_State
properties.claim: AlarmsStateChanged event SHALL contain current AlarmsActive and AlarmsSuppressed values.
properties.formal: ∀event. gen_event(AlarmsStateChanged, event) ⟹ event.AlarmsActive == current(AlarmsActive) ∧ event.AlarmsSuppressed == current(AlarmsSuppressed)
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.8.1.1, Page 95: 'This field SHALL indicate the state of active alarm modes, as indicated by the AlarmsActive attribute, at the time the event was generated.'

violation.description: If events contained stale or incorrect state data, controllers would have wrong view of alarm state.
violation.attack_vector: Implementation bug sending incorrect state in events
violation.physical_impact: Controller makes decisions based on incorrect alarm state

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, event:alarmEvent ⊢ event.AlarmsActive == read(device, AlarmsActive)

properties.name: No_Suppression_Duration_Limit
properties.claim: There is NO specification requirement for maximum suppression duration or auto-unsuppression.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7.1, Pages 94: No duration limit specified for suppression

violation.description: Once an alarm is suppressed, it remains suppressed indefinitely until the trigger condition clears. There is no automatic unsuppression timer. A suppressed alarm could remain suppressed for days while maintaining the hazardous trigger condition.
violation.attack_vector: Suppress alarm once, hazardous condition persists indefinitely without physical alert
violation.physical_impact: Water leak continues silently for days; broken window alarm suppressed indefinitely

properties.proverifQuery: N/A - Specification gap

properties.name: Suppression_Clears_On_Trigger_Clear
properties.claim: AlarmsSuppressed SHALL be cleared when sensor trigger condition clears.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6.5, Page 93: 'When the sensor is no longer triggered, this attribute SHALL be set to the unsuppressed state, by setting the bit to 0, for all supported alarm modes.'

violation.description: If suppressed state persisted after trigger cleared, the next trigger would immediately be suppressed without user action.
violation.attack_vector: N/A - this is proper behavior specification
violation.physical_impact: None - defines proper state reset behavior

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId ⊢ event trigger_cleared(device) ==> suppressed_bits(device) == 0

properties.name: BooleanState_Cluster_Coordination
properties.claim: Boolean State Configuration cluster operates in coordination with Boolean State cluster on same endpoint.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8, Page 90: 'This cluster is used to configure a boolean sensor'

violation.description: Boolean State Configuration without associated Boolean State cluster would be meaningless configuration with no actual sensor state.
violation.attack_vector: Device configuration error
violation.physical_impact: None - device type conformance issue

properties.proverifQuery: query endpoint:endpointId ⊢ has_cluster(endpoint, BooleanStateConfiguration) ==> has_cluster(endpoint, BooleanState)

properties.name: AlarmsActive_Reflects_BooleanState_Trigger
properties.claim: AlarmsActive bits SHALL only be set when Boolean State cluster's StateValue indicates triggered condition.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.6.4, Page 93: 'When the sensor is no longer triggered, this attribute SHALL be set to the inactive state'

violation.description: If AlarmsActive could be set without actual sensor trigger, false alarms would be generated. Conversely, if alarms didn't clear when trigger clears, stale alarms would persist.
violation.attack_vector: Timing race between sensor state and alarm state
violation.physical_impact: False alarms causing alarm fatigue, or missed alarm clearing

properties.proverifQuery: query device:deviceId, bit:alarmBit ⊢ active_bit(device, bit) == true ==> triggered(device)

properties.name: No_Physical_Presence_Verification
properties.claim: Neither SuppressAlarm nor EnableDisableAlarm commands require physical presence verification.
properties.spec_source: Section 1.8.7, Page 94: No physical presence or timed interaction requirement

violation.description: Remote alarm suppression/disable without physical presence creates risk of unauthorized silence of safety alerts. Unlike some safety clusters, there is no timed interaction or hardware button requirement.
violation.attack_vector: Malicious remote suppression without user awareness
violation.physical_impact: Safety alerts suppressed without occupant knowledge

properties.proverifQuery: N/A - Specification design concern

assumptions.statement: Matter fabric provides authenticated communication channels.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Access control properties fail; any node could invoke commands

assumptions.statement: Operate privilege indicates a trusted user or automation in the home.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Operate privilege compromise enables alarm suppression/disable

assumptions.statement: Sensor hardware correctly detects trigger conditions.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: False negatives or false positives regardless of software configuration

assumptions.statement: All events are delivered reliably to subscribed clients.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Controllers miss alarm state change notifications

spec_gaps.description: No maximum suppression duration specified. Suppression persists indefinitely until trigger clears.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Water leak or contact sensor alarm could be suppressed for days/weeks if trigger condition persists (e.g., slow leak, window left ajar)

spec_gaps.description: No rate limiting on SuppressAlarm or EnableDisableAlarm commands.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Command flooding attacks possible; rapid state toggling

spec_gaps.description: No physical presence verification or timed interaction requirement for alarm suppression/disable.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Compromised remote controller can silence all alarms without any physical verification

spec_gaps.description: EnableDisableAlarm uses only Operate privilege (not Manage/Admin) for completely disabling safety alarms.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Low privilege barrier for disabling all alarm functionality. Any Operate-level node can permanently disable alarms.

spec_gaps.description: Disabling an alarm silently clears active and suppressed states with no notification of the clear reason.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Active alarm conditions can be silently cleared by disable operation. AlarmsStateChanged event generated but does not distinguish between trigger-clear and disable-clear.

spec_gaps.description: No audit trail or notification when CurrentSensitivityLevel is changed.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Sensitivity can be reduced silently; no event generated for sensitivity changes

property.claim: AlarmsActive state SHALL be consistent with Boolean State StateValue trigger condition.

cross_cluster_properties.violation_scenario: Timing race between StateValue change and AlarmsActive update could create brief inconsistency

safety_critical_properties.failure_mode: Remote suppression of water leak, contact sensor, or other safety alerts without physical presence
Complete disabling of all alarm functionality by any Operate-privilege node
safety_critical_properties.spec_mitigation: UNSPECIFIED - Only basic Operate privilege required

safety_critical_properties.failure_mode: Indefinite suppression of active hazard alerts
